title: My case — private zone
lead: Upload results to keep them in one private place for your care team. We redact personal identifiers, extract clinical facts in memory, and destroy the original immediately.
uploadSectionSub: Powered by Gemma 4 · in-memory processing · original never stored on disk
gateSubtitleNeedsRole: Choose the patient / caregiver role to unlock your private upload zone.
gateSubtitleWrongRole: My case is for patient and caregiver accounts. Clinician accounts can browse guidelines and suggest doctors elsewhere.
gateSubtitleSignIn: Upload your results to share them conveniently with specialists. By contributing an anonymized version for AI analysis, you directly support research on new treatments and more precise diagnostics.
gateIntroAfter: , your records are a rare clinical signal — useful for your care team and for building better evidence for similar families.
promise0: Keep discharge summaries and lab results in one private place — ready to share with specialists when you choose
promise1: Gemma 4 strips personal identifiers (names, IDs, exact dates, addresses) before anything is saved
promise2: Only anonymized clinical facts persist — the original is processed in memory and never stored on disk
promise3: Anonymized facts help AI analyze patterns and speed research on new treatments and sharper diagnostics
promise3For: Anonymized facts help AI analyze patterns and speed research on new treatments and sharper diagnostics for
promise4: You stay in control: facts are private to your account; sharing with verified clinicians is opt-in
comingSoon1: Custom research runs tuned to your mutation and phenotype
comingSoon2: Trial alerts when a study matches your case
comingSoon3: Optional sharing with verified clinicians — off by default
gateHintBefore: Use the role picker to continue — choose
gateFooter: Guidelines, doctors, and trials on GeneGuidelines stay available without an account.
originalDestroyedBody: The file lived in server memory for the duration of one request handler. It never touched disk, never reached a backup, and is gone now.
hashMementoNote: The hash is the only memento. Two uploads of the same document produce the same hash — without anyone needing to know what was inside.
blurbPrefix: Your medical data is treated as a
blurbBold: deposit for seconds, not for storage
blurbSuffix: . The document arrives over an encrypted channel, is processed only in the memory of an isolated worker, and the original is destroyed right after the extraction — it never touches disk, and never reaches any backup.
supportedFormats: Supported: .txt · .md · .pdf · .jpg · .png · ≤ 30 MB · scans and photos are read by OCR · add several at once
extractedFoot: These facts — and only these facts — are the input to the next AI guideline draft for this disease.
